zh.lifeSummary: Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly於1101年出生在英國英格蘭戴文。她和Nicholas de Kelly至少有1個兒子。她去世在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly于1101年出生在英国英格兰戴文。她和Nicholas de Kelly至少有1个儿子。她去世在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Kelly
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Aileen, John Patrick, Siobhan, Liam, Brigid, Donovan, Eamon, Parnell, Aidan, Clancy.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Ceallaigh, or, in some areas, Mac Ceallaigh ‘descendant (or son) of Ceallach’, an ancient Irish personal name, originally a byname meaning ‘bright-headed’, later understood as ‘frequenting churches’ (Irish ceall). There are several early Irish saints who bore this name. Kelly is now the most common of all surnames in Ireland.

Scottish: habitational name from either the lands of Kelly, near Arbroath (Angus), Kellie in Carnbee (Fife), or Kelly in Methlick (Aberdeenshire). Each of these placenames is from Pictish celli or Gaelic coille ‘wood’ + the placename forming suffix -in. Alternatively, from the lands of Kelloe in the barony of Hume (Berwickshire), whose name is of uncertain origin.

variants.name: Kelley
meanings: Irish (Offaly) and English: variant of   Kelly  .

variants.name: Kilkelly
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Bridie.
Irish (Galway): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Ceallaigh ‘son of the devotee of (Saint) Ceallach’ (see Kelly ).

variants.name: Killeen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Donovan, Kieran, Liam.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Cillín ‘descendant of Cillín’, a pet form of the personal name Ceallach (see Kelly ). The name was borne by various early Irish saints, including the leader of a 7th-century mission to Franconia and Thuringia, hence the popularity of the personal name Kilian in medieval central Europe.

variants.name: Kellis
meanings: Scottish: patronymic based on   Kelly   as a personal name. Compare   Kellison  .
Greek: descriptive nickname for a bald man, from kelis ‘bald’, a word of Turkish origin (from kel ‘bald’).
History: As a surname of Scottish origin, this has been established in NC since the 18th century.

variants.name: Kellie
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Kelly  .

variants.name: Kellison
meanings: Scottish: patronymic based on   Kelly   as a personal name.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

variants.name: Nicolas
meanings: From Late Greek. See Nicholas .

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly was born in 1101, in Devon, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with Nicholas de Kelly. She died in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly est née en 1101, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Nicholas de Kelly. Elle est décédée dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly wurde 1101 in Devon, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hatte mit Nicholas de Kelly mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly è nata il nel 1101 nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Nicholas de Kelly. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.of Kelley, Devon, England: イギリスイギリスデボン
ja.lifeSummary: Mrs. Nicholas de Kellyは1101年にイギリスイギリスデボンで生まれた。彼女はNicholas de Kellyとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly(은)는 1101년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Nicholas de Kelly(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly nasceu em 1101, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Nicholas de Kelly. Ela faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly родилась в 1101 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Nicholas de Kelly. Она умерла в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly nació en 1101, en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Nicholas de Kelly. Murió en su pueblo.
